rationale: The cited 2001 JMR paper is directly about modeling and quantifying multiple response styles (acquiescence, disacquiescence, extreme/midpoint/noncontingent responding) and their contamination of scale scores, which closely matches the citing 2010 paper’s core problem of separating response-style from content effects. Given this strong construct and methodological continuity in a cross-national marketing-survey setting, this citation is plausibly foundational/substantive rather than an incidental mention.
